title: Starches and Thickeners
content:
STARCHES & THICKENERS

Cornstarch: Refined starch from corn endosperm. Substitute 1 tablespoon cornstarch for 2 tablespoons flour for thickening. Mix with sugar before adding to liquid to avoid lumps.

Tapioca/Arrowroot: Good for clear glazes and frozen sauces. Substitute 1½ teaspoons for 1 tablespoon flour.

Potato Flour/Starch: Use in soups, gravies, breads, cakes. Substitute 1 tablespoon for 2 tablespoons flour for thickening; ⅝ cup for 1 cup flour in baking.

MEASURING FLOURS
- Never pack flour when measuring
- Spoon lightly into cup until overflowing
- Level with straight edge
- Sift before measuring for cakes and cookies
- Coarse flours need not be sifted

CORNSTARCH COOKING METHOD
1. Mix cornstarch with sugar (if used) or make paste with cold liquid
2. Add gradually to hot (not boiling) liquid while stirring
3. Cook 8-12 minutes to 185°F, stirring constantly until thickened
4. Cover and cook undisturbed 10 minutes to complete gelatinization
5. If adding eggs: beat eggs, add small amount of hot mixture, return to pan, cook gently 2 minutes
